message:
test(lexical/cass-compat): CJK bigram decompose matches legacy reference implementation

Add a targeted equivalence test that pins `CjkBigramDecompose` against
an inlined reference implementation, so a future refactor of the
bigram filter can't silently diverge from the on-disk contract set by
`CASS_SCHEMA_HASH`.

- `legacy_cjk_bigram_output(text)` is the reference: if the input is
  empty, contains any non-CJK char, or is a single CJK char, it
  returns `[text]` unchanged. Otherwise it emits bigrams of
  consecutive CJK chars (chars[i] + chars[i+1]) for i in
  0..len-1.
- `cjk_bigram_decompose_matches_legacy_reference` builds a
  `TextAnalyzer` pipeline of `RegexTokenizer(r"[^\s]+")` +
  `CjkBigramDecompose`, drives it over a representative sample set
  (empty-ish `"搜"`, 2-char `"搜索"`, 4-char `"搜索引擎"`,
  Hiragana `"あいうえお"`, Hangul `"가나다"`, pure ASCII `"hello"`,
  mixed `"bd-q3fy"`, ASCII+CJK boundary cases `"abc搜索"` /
  `"搜索abc"`, and interleaved `"中A文"`), and asserts the filter's
  token stream equals the reference output for every sample.

Test is colocated with the existing `cass_query_tests` module so it
runs under the standard `cargo test -p frankensearch-lexical`
invocation without additional features or harness setup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
